title: Lesson 372
transcript: ðŸŽµ ðŸŽµ We just completed talking about software-defined networks and how through this centralized software and intelligent application, we can basically control everything that's happening within the network. And this is really the foundation for what has given rise to really cool technologies like cloud and so on. What's been showing up on the exam lately is something related to this and the usage of what you see here on this slide called northbound APIs and southbound APIs. So if you think about those components that we just talked about, in order to allow things to happen across a network, you know we have what's called the control plane that makes the decisions right that's the decision maker and then of course the data plane is what forwards the actual packets across that decision that's been made by the control plane And because of software-defined networking and really virtualization, especially if you look at things like cloud environments, we've got really intelligent applications that control everything that's happening across a network. and across an architecture. For example, across the Cloud environment, we can call that the management plane. So here's where northbound and southbound APIs are actually used. First of all, an API, as you know stands for application programming interface but they are standards by which we can allow components of architectures to be able to communicate with each other and pass information right so if you think about the components that make up any network here you've got the application plane that really controls everything. You've got the control plane that really makes the decisions as to where, for example, packets and datagrams need to be able to go to. And then of course, it's the data plane that actually forwards those packets. Those components need to be connected and they need to talk to each other, and that's what APIs do. So if you look at the control plane as being the reference point here, because it's in the middle, then we can understand very plainly the difference between northbound APIs and southbound APIs. If you look at the control plane as the reference point, if you want to connect the control plane to a higher level component, which is obviously the application plane, you're going north. you're going up. And so that's what northbound APIs do. They connect the control plane to a higher level component called the application plane. Because you're going north, then it's called northbound APIs. And if you're connecting, for example, the control plane to a lower level component, which means you're going south, then that's what southbound APIs are. do they connect the control plane to a lower level component so the direction that you're going always using the control plane as a reference point is the difference between northbound apis and southbound apis Thank you.
